rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE BECK VS. TONG HO HSING INDUSTRIAL CO LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Oiton For A Good Faith Settlement Determination
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 8, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANTs TONG HO HSING INDUSTRIAL CO LTD & TRANS NATIONAL TRADING, INC.'s motion For A Good Faith Settlement Determination. Denied. Given the evidence concerning the likelihood of liability, the likely allocation of any liability, and the absence of collusion, the contesting parties have not shown that the settlement is outside the ballpark under the Tech-Bilt factors. A continuance for discovery is not warranted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JESSICA JIMENEZ VS. MENZIES AVIATION, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 8, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANTs MENZIES AVIATION, INC. &MENZIES AVIATION GROUP (USA), INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrer to the first through tenth causes of action on the grounds that Plaintiff cannot state a viable claim is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to plead, if she can do so in good faith, facts sufficient to state a claim under Morillion v. Royal Packing Co. (2000) 22 Cal.4th 575 and Overton v. Walt Disney Co. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 263. As currently pled, the allegations in the Complaint are unclear. Plaintiff pleads that employees of Defendants were required to park in designated lots and take a shuttle to their working location, but elsewhere pleads facts which tend to indicate that Plaintiffs were permitted to take other forms of transportation to work. The demurrer on all other grounds is overruled. Regarding the possibility of class certification on both the commuting theory and the uniform theory, Plaintiff's allegations show a reasonable probability that Plaintiff can establish a community of interest and an ascertainable class. (See Canon USA, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 1, 5; Alvarez v. The May Dept. Stores Co. (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1223, 1231-32.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN G. PROTAS VS. JASON MOORE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Vacate Sister-State Judgmnt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 8, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT JASON MOORE's motion To Vacate Sister-State Judgment. Granted. The sister-state judgment is vacated. (CCP § 1710.40(a).) A single sale of a car on eBay is insufficient to subject Defendant to local personal jurisdiction. (Boschetto v. Hansing (9th Cir. 2008) 539 F.3d 1011, 1018-1019.) Plaintiffs have not presented sufficient evidence to establish personal jurisdiction of Defendant in Arizona. The alternative relief for a stay is denied as moot in light of this ruling. (CCP § 1710.50.) rulings.Case Title: BEVERLY BERNELL MYRES VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Physical Examination Of Pltf And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, July 8, 2013, Line 1: Defendant SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S Motion to Compel Physical Examination of Plaintiff and Request for Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the motion. No costs or sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TERILYN JOE et al VS. 1520 TAYLOR STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 8, 2013, Line 14, Motion For Summary Adjudication. The Court will hear the motion for summary adjudication before the motion to amend. The Court denies the motion because defendant failed to shift the burden. The notice of motion indicates that defendant is seeking to adjudicate the cause of action for declaratory relief but it does not address Plaintiffs' claims regarding "related building systems." (Complaint, 17:4-5). In other words, the motion does not completely dispose of the cause of action. Even if the Court concludes that the motion also seeks to adjudicate an issue of duty regarding the windows, the motion fails. Defendant is correct that it would not be responsible for the windows if they were defective. Under CC 1364 and Dover Village Assn. v. Jennison (2010) 191 Cal.App.4th 123, 126, the obligation to repair a defective window would fall upon Plaintiffs. The Court rejects plaintiffs' argument that the CC&Rs modified the statutory scheme. At RFA 11, Plaintiffs admit that the CC&Rs do not modify the statutory duties and that admission is conclusive. See CCP 2033.410(a). Nevertheless, defendants may still have a duty to repair and replace the windows if its tortious conduct in managing the general common areas (as opposed to the exclusive use common areas) caused the damage. The record is devoid of evidence to resolve that factual issue regarding causation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TERILYN JOE et al VS. 1520 TAYLOR STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 8, 2013, Line 14, PLAINTIFFs TERILYN JOE & LINDA LUOMA's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. The Court will hear the motion to amend after the motion for summary adjudication. The motion to amend is granted. The supplemental declaration of S. Clinton Woods substantially complies with CRC 3.1324. The proposed amendments simply amplify the allegations about the "related building systems." Plaintiffs are not changing the tenor of the case or undercutting the motion for summary adjudication. The Court grants leave to amend for plaintiffs to plead the misappropriation cause of action. The motion to amend is not the vehicle to resolve the underlying merits of that claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A. VS. HEATHER E CASSELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Deem Matters Admitted And Req For Sanctions; Notice Of Non-Appearance; Ntc Of Lodgment
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, July 8, 2013, Line 4: Plaintiff CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'S Motion to Deem Matters Admitted and Request for Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the unopposed motions. $3,000 in sanctions are awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SCULPT GARDENS INC., A CORPORATION VS. MARK MARROCCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To Amend Complaintq
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 8, 2013, Line 17, PLAINTIFF SCULPT GARDENS INC.'s motion For Leave To Amend Complaint. The motion to amend is granted. Allowing the amendment is not prejudicial. Defendant does not conclusively demonstrate that the new claims fail as a matter of law and the better practice is to have those issues raised by demurrer or a motion for summary judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CONSUMER ADVOCAY GROUP, INC. VS. NEXT PRODUCTS (USA), LLC A NEW JERSEY LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 8, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANTs THE TJX COMPANIES, INC. & T.J. MAXX OF CA, L.L.C's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants' Motion to Strike Plaintiff's Complaint is denied, as it is improper. A general demurrer is the proper vehicle to challenge Plaintiff's standing for deficiencies in its Proposition 65 pre-suit notice. (See CCP § 430.10(a); Yeroushalmi v. Miramar Sheraton (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 738, 740, 750.) A demurrer is also the proper vehicle to challenge the sufficiency of Plaintiff's pleadings. See CCP § 430.10(e). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CONSUMER ADVOCAY GROUP, INC. VS. NEXT PRODUCTS (USA), LLC A NEW JERSEY LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 8, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANTs THE TJX COMPANIES, INC. & T.J. MAXX OF CA, L.L.C's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to Plaintiff's Complaint is overruled. Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Plaintiff's pre-suit notice adequately complies with the requirements in 27 Cal. Code of Regulations §§ 25903(b)(2)(A), and 25903(b)(2)(D). The Office of Environmental Health Hazard Assessment's Final Statement of Reasons explicitly states that a Proposition 65 pre-suit notice is sufficiently specific if it identifies the category of products that will be the subject of the action. (See Declaration of Shoor in Support of Defendants' Demurrer, Motion to Strike, Request for Judicial Notice 4, Exh. C, pg. 10.). Plaintiff's pre-suit notice describes with sufficient specificity the nature and category of items allegedly sold in violation of the law - "Computer Cameras". Moreover, Plaintiff provides an exemplar - the "Smart Webcam®" - which further specifies the nature of the alleged violating consumer products. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: L&A WONG FAMILY PROPERTY ASSOCIATION LLC, et al VS. HAROLD WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Compelling Binding Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 8, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANTs HAROLD WONG & WILLIAM YEE AND ASSOCIATES' motion For An Order Compelling Binding Arbitration. The motion is denied. The Court refuses to compel arbitration based upon CCP 1281.2(c). William Yee & Associates is not a party to the arbitration agreement. Piecemeal litigation of plaintiff's claims against defendants could lead to inconsistent results. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
